project.name: tsnum
project.summary: Foundation TypeScript numerical computing library family moving toward a NumPy-compatible public contract with TypeScript, native, and WASM backend paths, benchmarks, tests, and package exports.
goals: Provide a typed, functional-first numerical computing API for JavaScript and TypeScript consumers.
Move the package family toward the @sylphx/numpy public contract with NumPy-compatible spelling, array semantics, and explicit backend boundaries.
Keep TypeScript, native, and WASM backend behavior coherent through package exports, tests, and benchmarks.
Publish only documented package exports and performance claims that can be reproduced by repository-local evidence.
nonGoals: Own NumPy upstream behavior, scientific correctness beyond the documented package contract, or downstream application modeling decisions.
Own consumer product numerical policy, visualization, storage, or UI behavior.
Publish enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, org rulesets, rollout issue reconciliation, or shared CI and release policy.

owns.name: tsnum-core-package
owns.description: The tsnum package exports, TypeScript implementation, tests, and documented numerical API behavior.

owns.name: tsnum-wasm-backend
owns.description: Rust/WASM backend package and compatibility expectations for accelerated numerical operations.

owns.name: tsnum-native-backend-path
owns.description: Native BLAS backend integration point and compatibility expectations for Python parity hot paths.

owns.name: tsnum-benchmark-evidence
owns.description: Repository-local benchmark and performance documentation used to support package performance claims.

doesNotOwn: NumPy upstream behavior, consumer scientific modeling decisions, or downstream application numerical policy.
Visualization, persistence, UI, or product-specific workflows built on top of numerical arrays.
Enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, rollout issue reconciliation, org rulesets, or shared workflow implementation.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema, standards, and rollout audit

forbiddenCouplings: Do not require consumers to import internal source paths instead of documented package exports.
Do not encode one consumer product's numerical policy or dataset assumptions as hidden library behavior.
Do not publish performance claims that lack reproducible benchmark evidence in this repository.
Do not publish enterprise process policy from this repository instead of SylphxAI/doctrine.

delivery.deployPath: No repo-local CI or release workflow is declared; package changes currently rely on local Bun/Turbo, TypeScript, test, benchmark, and changeset evidence before publish.
delivery.productionProof: Published package changes require build/test evidence, benchmark evidence for performance-sensitive changes, npm/package readback after release, and consumer smoke proof for affected exports.

gaps.description: Declare pull-request validation for install, lint, typecheck, tests, build, WASM backend compatibility, and benchmark smoke before full adoption.
Document package release, npm readback, changelog, and consumer smoke evidence required after publishing tsnum.
Turn performance claims and WASM fallback expectations into a stable benchmark contract or generated report.
